chunk_summary: The Minimum Clinic Ready WASH Action Package outlines practical, measurable interventions to address infection-driven undernutrition in pediatric populations, integrating hygiene, sanitation, and food safety with diarrhoea care protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of WASH in mitigating infection-driven undernutrition in children.
Learn the components of the Minimum Clinic Ready WASH Action Package for pediatric care.
Integrate WASH counseling with standard diarrhoea management protocols.
Identify measurable behavior changes to improve environmental cleanliness and hygiene.
key_facts: Repeated enteric infections contribute to appetite loss, malabsorption, and inflammation, worsening undernutrition.
Standard diarrhoea management includes ORS plus zinc for 14 days, continued feeding, and hygiene counseling.
Safe drinking water should be stored in covered, narrow-mouth containers without hand-dipping.
Handwashing with soap is critical at key times: after defecation, cleaning a child, before food preparation, feeding, and eating.
Sanitation practices include consistent toilet use and safe child feces disposal, avoiding open drains.
BabyWASH concepts emphasize clean play spaces, reduced infant contact with soil and animal feces, and keeping animals away from feeding areas.
reference_quote: Large trials have shown limited linear growth improvement from basic household WASH packages, but policy guidance explains that incomplete environmental cleanliness likely explains the gap. (WHO and UNICEF position paper, 2019)
